text: A basic kit for treating minor cuts, scrapes, and injuries.
A portable lock that secures a door without requiring hardware installation.
A gel-based pepper spray with an adjustable, flip-top nozzle.
A device used to measure blood alcohol content (BAC) from a breath sample.
A personal alarm that emits a loud siren and flashing light when activated.
A nasal spray that reverses the effects of an opioid overdose.
A bar that wedges under a door to prevent it from being opened from outside.
A retractable ladder used to exit through an upper-floor window.
A barrier device used to perform rescue breathing safely.
A small, adjustable flashlight for use in low-light or no-light situations.
Test strips used to detect fentanyl contamination in a substance before use.
A pen that also functions as a self-defense tool and glass breaker.
A tracking device used to locate a bag, backpack, or set of keys.
The master guide with scenario-by-scenario instructions for each emergency covered in the plan.
A keychain with a retractable cord for keeping keys or small items within reach.
A small bag used to carry smaller kit items securely.
A storage bin used to organize and store the full kit.
